title: How to get a police certificate – Brazil - Canada.ca
content:
## How to get a police certificate – Brazil
If you need to give your fingerprints for a police certificate, this isn’t the same as giving your biometrics (fingerprints and photo) for an application.

## Name of the document you need
Certidão de Antecedentes Criminais, issued by the Brazilian Federal Police

## Police certificates and IRCC requirements
You must provide a police certificate when you apply for permanent residence in Canada.
If you have Brazilian ID cards ( Cédula de Identidade de Estrangeiro [CIE] and Cadastro de Pessoa Fisica [CPF]), you must contact the Brazilian Federal Police to obtain a Certidão de Antecedentes Criminais. Visit the Brazilian Federal Police website (in Portuguese only) for instructions and to apply online.
If you do not have Brazilian ID cards ( Cédula de Identidade de Estrangeiro [CIE] and Cadastro de Pessoa Fisica [CPF]), you will not be able to request a certificate online. You should apply in person at your nearest Polícia federal office. Visit the Polícia federal website (in Portuguese only) for a complete list of offices.

## Special considerations
Brazilian state certificates are no longer accepted. Only national certificates issued by the Brazilian Federal Police are accepted.
Directorate General – DG Alexandre Ramagen SAS Qd. 06, LT 9/10 Headquarters Ed ZIP Code 70.037-900 – brasília /DF Telephone: (61) 2024-8000 Email: dg@dpf.gov.br
